steps.title: Find the growth factor
steps.content: A mass that is $125\%$ greater than the previous mass is $100\%+125\%=225\%$, or $2.25$, times the previous mass. Since this happens every $5$ hours, the exponent counts $5$-hour periods.

steps.title: Work backward from hour fifteen
steps.content:
After $15$ hours, there have been $15/5=3$ growth periods. If $a$ is the starting mass, then
$$a(2.25)^3=637.88$$
$$a=\frac{637.88}{(2.25)^3}=56.00$$

steps.title: Write the model
steps.content: Use the starting mass and growth factor with the exponent $x/5$: $$A=56.00(2.25)^{\frac{x}{5}}$$ This matches the equation with initial value $56.00$ and growth factor $2.25$. The answer is C.
